doc.title: Lessor — Year-End Interest Accrual
doc.event: Continuing Hale Equipment's sales-type lease: lease receivable of $178,855 at commencement, first $50,000 payment received that day, 8% implicit rate. Prepare Hale's December 31 entry to accrue interest.

lines.why: Interest runs on the receivable balance AFTER the commencement-day receipt.
lines.trap: 8% × 178,855 = 14,308 double-counts — the day-one receipt already reduced the balance.

lines.why: Earned by the passage of time, collected with the next payment.

memorize.body: Lessor interest accrues on the NET receivable after any beginning-of-period receipt — the mirror image of the lessee's interest calc.

questions.prompt: Receivable $178,855, $50,000 received at commencement, 8% rate. Year-1 interest revenue = ?

distractors.feedback: You applied 8% to the pre-receipt balance. The day-one collection dropped it to 128,855 first.
8% × the payment is meaningless — interest runs on the receivable BALANCE.
Interest is earned by time passing, not by cash arriving — accrue it at year-end even though the next receipt is January 1.

build.scaffold: One two-line accrual. The only work is picking the right base: post-receipt balance.
